deficits.missing: The MFU worked numbers are wrong by 2-3x and physically impossible. The compute_mfu example claims 7,000 tok/s/GPU on a 70B model 'typically prints ~42%' — actual value is 149% vs the 1979-TFLOPS peak used (and 297% vs the correct dense bf16 peak of ~989 TFLOPS; 1979 is the 2:4-sparsity figure). The 70B memory-budget example says 'need >= 2.4e6 tokens/s for 50% MFU: ~4,688 tok/s per GPU' — correct is ~1.21e6 tok/s cluster / ~2,356 per GPU against the sparse peak, ~1,178 against dense. 'A well-tuned 70B run achieves 5,000-7,000 tok/s per GPU, corresponding to MFU of 49-66%' is off by ~3x; realistic is ~1,000-2,000 tok/s/GPU.
deficits.fix: Use the dense bf16 H100 peak (989 TFLOPS) for MFU denominators; recompute all worked numbers: 50% MFU on 70B corresponds to ~1,180 tok/s/GPU; state realistic 70B throughput as ~800-1,500 tok/s/GPU at 35-55% MFU; fix the compute_mfu example comment.

deficits.missing: The global-batch-size guidance formula 'GBS ≈ C_critical × S / T where S is sequence length and T is vocabulary size' is meaningless — critical batch size has nothing to do with vocabulary size, and the formula is dimensionally incoherent.
deficits.fix: Replace with the gradient-noise-scale framing (McCandlish et al., B_crit ≈ gradient noise scale) or simply state empirical GBS ranges (1-4M tokens/step for >10B models) and cross-link to the LR/batch-size chapter, which the surrounding prose already gestures at.

deficits.missing: The ring_attention_forward pseudocode produces NaN for causal=True: when a received KV chunk lies entirely in the future of a query row (e.g. rank 0 receives rank N-1's chunk at step 1), the entire score row is set to -inf, so torch.softmax returns NaN and the NaN propagates into output_acc even though exp_new=0 (NaN*0=NaN).
deficits.fix: Skip fully-masked KV blocks (if kv_global_start > q_global_start + T_local - 1: rotate and continue) or guard the softmax/merge against all--inf rows; note that production kernels skip these blocks entirely, which is also the source of the causal load imbalance discussed in 3.6.

deficits.missing: TP coverage omits the vocab-parallel embedding and the parallel (vocab-sharded) cross-entropy loss — the embedding and LM head are the largest single matrices in the model, and an end-to-end TP transformer cannot be assembled without sharding them (Megatron's VocabParallelEmbedding masking + all-reduce trick, and the max-subtraction + two-all-reduce parallel softmax cross-entropy).
deficits.fix: Add a short section with code for VocabParallelEmbedding (mask out-of-range token ids, zero their rows, all-reduce) and vocab-parallel cross-entropy (local max, all-reduce max, local sum-exp, all-reduce sum, gather target logit), with exact shapes.

deficits.missing: Prose swaps FlashAttention's standard notation: 'the running max ℓ and running sum m' — ℓ is the running sum and m the running max, and the chapter's own code uses m_run as the max and l_run as the sum, contradicting the sentence.
deficits.fix: Change to 'the running max m and running sum ℓ' to match both the FlashAttention papers and the code below it.

deficits.missing: Two smaller formula inconsistencies: (a) ZeRO-3 memory is given as (2P+12P)/DP = 14P/DP, omitting the 2P of gradients and contradicting the chapter's own 16-bytes-per-param accounting a few sections later; (b) the interleaved bubble is simplified '(PP-1)/(PP-1+v·m) ≈ 1/(v·m)', dropping the (PP-1) numerator — correct first-order form is (PP-1)/(v·m); (c) the FLOPs/token formula prints a stray '/1' denominator and its attention term (12·L·d·S) disagrees with the code's 4·L·d·S (forward-only vs fwd+bwd).
deficits.fix: Use 16P/DP for ZeRO-3, write the interleaved bubble as (1/v)·(p-1)/(m+p-1) as chapter 3.6 correctly does, remove the '/1', and make the code's attention term 12·L·d·S to match the 6P fwd+bwd convention.

deficits.missing: The expert-parallel MoE sketch relies on undefined helpers (bucket_tokens_by_rank, weighted_scatter_add, run_local_experts) and uses torch.empty_like(send_buf)/empty_like(local_out) for all_to_all_single, which silently assumes equal per-rank token counts — the variable-split-size handling (input_split_sizes/output_split_sizes from an exchanged counts tensor) is the actual hard part of EP.
deficits.fix: Show the counts exchange (all_to_all on a per-rank count tensor) and pass explicit input/output split sizes to all_to_all_single, or explicitly defer to the MoE chapter if a complete implementation lives there.

deficits.missing: No correctness-verification recipe for any of the parallelism schemes: nothing tells the reader to check that a TP=2 (or PP=2, CP=2) run reproduces the TP=1 loss/logits bit-for-bit (up to reduction order) on 2 GPUs before scaling up — the standard sanity harness for distributed implementations.
deficits.fix: Add a short 'verify before you scale' box: fixed seed, tiny model, compare single-GPU logits/loss vs TP=2/PP=2/CP=2 with torch.allclose tolerances, plus the KV-head divisibility and stage-balance checks as assertions.

buildable_reasoning: The deliverable — choose and justify a full parallelism plan for a given model and cluster — is genuinely buildable from the book alone. Chapter 3.6 gives the complete decision machinery: exact column/row TP partitioning with shapes and working f/g autograd code, the bubble formula (p-1)/(m+p-1) for GPipe and 1F1B with a runnable 1F1B schedule driver, the interleaved 1/v improvement, ring attention with online-softmax accumulation, EP all-to-all structure, the per-axis communication summary table (collective, frequency, tensor moved, link needed), the placement principle (TP inside NVLink, PP/DP across fabric), and a fully worked 70B/512-GPU example (TP=8 x PP=8 x DP=8 with memory arithmetic and bubble check). Chapter 3.7 adds the systematic 5-step workflow (fit, pick TP, pick PP with m >= 19(PP-1), fill DP, tune GBS/GAS), process-group construction code, a complete launch script with all hyperparameters, and profiling guidance. A competent reader can produce and defend a correct plan. The caveat: the MFU/throughput validation numbers in 3.7 are wrong by 2-3x, so a reader sanity-checking their plan's expected throughput against the book would be badly misled — this blocks a grade of 4 but not the plan-choosing deliverable itself, since the memory accounting, bubble math, and placement logic that determine the plan are correct.
evidence: 3.6 gives exact TP shapes: 'A = [A_1,...,A_t] where each A_i in R^{h x h'/t}' and working ColumnParallelLinear/RowParallelLinear with the f/g autograd functions placing all-reduces correctly
3.6: 'each transformer block needs exactly two all-reduces in the forward pass... and two in the backward' — the Megatron f/g formulation, correct
3.6 bubble formula '(p-1)/(m+p-1)' with worked numbers (p=8, m=64 -> ~10%), 1F1B memory bound 'at most p - s for stage s... independent of m', and interleaved '1/v · (p-1)/(m+p-1)' — all correct
3.6 runnable 1F1B driver with warmup = p - stage - 1, steady-state, cooldown phases
3.6 ring attention code with online-softmax merge (correct rescaling corr = exp(m_run - m_new)) plus warnings on causal load imbalance and GQA KV-head/TP divisibility
3.6 worked example shards a 70B model onto 512 H100s step by step (16N bytes -> TP=8 -> PP=8 -> DP=8 -> bubble check) — exactly the unit's deliverable, done correctly
3.7 gives m >= 19(PP-1) for <5% bubble, process-group construction code, a complete 70B Megatron launch script, and Nsight profiling symptoms/fixes
BUG (3.7): '# typically prints ~42%' on an example that computes 7000 tok/s/GPU x 6·70e9 FLOPs / 1.979e15 = 149% MFU — impossible; dense-peak value would be 297%
BUG (3.7): 'Need >= 2.4e6 tokens/s for 50% MFU: ~4,688 tokens/s per GPU' — correct is 1.21e6 / ~2,356 per GPU against the (sparse) peak the chapter itself uses
BUG (3.7): 'GBS ≈ C_critical × S / T ... where T is vocabulary size' — dimensionally incoherent formula
BUG (3.13): causal ring-attention pseudocode sets whole score rows to -inf for future KV chunks, so torch.softmax yields NaN that propagates through the merge
1.9 provides the collectives substrate: 'All-Reduce = Reduce-Scatter followed by All-Gather', ring all-reduce 2·(n-1)/n·M bandwidth optimality, alpha-beta cost model
summary: Coverage of Parallelism II is strong and CS336-competitive: chapter 3.6 delivers buildable tensor parallelism (exact shapes, working column/row code with f/g autograd ops), pipeline parallelism (GPipe/1F1B/interleaved with correct bubble formulas and a runnable 1F1B schedule), ring attention with correct online-softmax merging, expert parallelism, and a correctly-worked 70B/512-GPU plan; chapter 3.7 adds the systematic plan-selection workflow, launch scripts, and profiling. The deliverable (choose and justify a parallelism plan) is achievable from the book alone. What blocks a 4: the MFU/throughput verification numbers in 3.7 are wrong by 2-3x (7,000 tok/s/GPU on 70B labeled '~42% MFU' is actually ~149-297%; the 50%-MFU token-rate target is doubled), a garbled critical-batch-size formula, a NaN-producing causal mask in the long-context ring-attention pseudocode, no vocab-parallel embedding/cross-entropy for end-to-end TP, and no verify-against-single-GPU test recipe.
